steps: derive a stable workspace identity from profileId, workspaceId, and instanceId
reject the canonical tmux session and any existing conflicting instanceId
capture before window, process, and tmux-session inventory
invoke the exact canonical launcher in explicit new-instance mode once
capture child stdout, stderr, exit code, and correlation ID
require exactly one new top-level WezTerm window
require a distinct frontend process and unique tmux session for the instance
invoke the same instanceId a second time
require convergence to the named instance without another window
classify duplicate-detected when one request creates two windows or one instanceId is visible in more than one window
emit result, English report, and handoff
proofCeiling: Workflow contract and synthetic fixtures only; no distinct process or tmux session is proven until a workstation run.
